name: US Unemployment Rate

book_quotes.quote: Figure 2.7 displays the path of the (official) unemployment rate from 1890 to 2010. It provides a vivid picture of the enormous impact that Great Depressions have on economic life.

Figure 2.7 US Unemployment Rate, 1890–2010. Civilian unemployment rate 1860–1970 from BEA (1966, Series B1-B2), and from 1948 to 2010 from the Economic Report of the President, http://www.gpo.gov/fdsys/browse/collection.action?collectionCode=ERP., table b-40.

primary_source.agency: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (historical) + Council of Economic Advisers / GPO (modern)
primary_source.publication: BEA (1966) Long Term Economic Growth Series B1–B2 (1860–1970); Economic Report of the President table B-40 (1948–2010)
primary_source.table_or_series_id: BEA LTEG B1–B2 spliced with ERP Table B-40

components.label: BEA (1966) LTEG Series B1–B2 (civilian unemployment rate)
Economic Report of the President Table B-40

concerns: Same underlying BLS CPS concept as ERP Table B-40; direct continuation.

methodology_notes: Composite splice of BEA LTEG B1–B2 with ERP Table B-40 per Appendix 2.1 (p. 764). Modern extension is seamless via FRED UNRATE (annual mean of monthly).
CD2 S009 dossier validated; pre-1948 LTEG estimates differ in methodology from modern CPS — historical comparability caveat.
open_questions: ERP archive URL has migrated to govinfo.gov; verify table numbering still maps to B-40 in current annual editions.
